title: Aureline graph topology-map view, ownership card, freshness/provenance strip, and list/table alternate contract
description: Boundary schema for the topology-map view family. Every topology surface (architecture-domain map, runtime-domain map, deployment-relation map, dependency map, ownership map, generated-artifact lineage map, provider-resource map) renders one topology_map_view_record from this contract; ownership cards render one ownership_card_record. Both records reuse graph_node_record / graph_edge_record ids verbatim (schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json) and reuse the provenance / confidence / anchor-drift contract (schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json, schemas/graph/confidence_rollup.schema.json) rather than minting bespoke topology-only certainty labels. Loaded scope, hidden-by-workset / hidden-by-policy / not-loaded counts, and imported-root markers are first-class so a topology can present a partial or policy-limited workset honestly instead of pretending the full workspace is loaded. Each view declares one topology_family_class so ownership, architecture-domain, runtime-domain, and deployment-relation maps stay distinguishable instead of flattening into one generic map. The presentation_mode axis covers map_canvas plus the list_alternate / table_alternate forms so the same record can drive a map view, a list view, or a table view without redefining topology identity. Raw absolute paths, raw provider URLs, raw email addresses, and raw credentials never cross this boundary; opaque ids and typed labels do. Adding a new topology_family_class, presentation_mode, legend_section_class, filter_class, ownership_card_section_class, export_format_class, or offered_action is additive-minor and bumps topology_map_view_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

topology_map_view_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the topology-map-view vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque equality-comparable string.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Never carries raw source bodies or raw URLs.

redaction_aware_text.description: Redaction-aware short text (≤ 480 graphemes). Surfaces render this verbatim. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, raw email addresses, or raw credentials.

topology_family_class.description: Frozen topology-family vocabulary. Every topology view names exactly one family so ownership, architecture-domain, runtime-domain, and deployment-relation maps stay distinguishable instead of collapsing into one generic 'graph map'. ownership_topology: CODEOWNERS / team / rotation map over owned_by edges. architecture_domain_topology: package / crate / module structure over depends_on / imports_module / contains edges. runtime_domain_topology: service / runtime host / kernel map over runs_in / hosted_by edges. deployment_relation_topology: deploy target / environment / region map over deployed_to edges. dependency_topology: pure depends_on walk (lockfile / package-resolver). generated_artifact_lineage_topology: generated_from / produces_artifact / consumes_artifact lineage map. provider_resource_topology: connected-provider object map. pack_mirrors_upstream_topology: mirrored upstream pack lineage. imported_root_topology: imported-root visibility map. workset_scope_topology: scope narrowing visualization. The family is the disclosure axis a reviewer reads to know which graph relations the map is summarising; surfaces never conflate two families into one render.

presentation_mode.description: Frozen presentation-mode vocabulary. The same topology_map_view_record drives a map canvas, a list alternate, a table alternate, or an outline-tree alternate without redefining topology identity. node_list_alternate and node_table_alternate render the same node entries the canvas would; edge_table_alternate renders the edges; outline_tree_alternate renders the contains / depends_on hierarchy. Surfaces never invent a private list or table mode.

loaded_scope_state.description: Frozen loaded-scope state vocabulary. The view never claims fully_loaded when any contributing root, workset, or policy overlay narrowed the scope. partial_workset_loaded: the active workset narrows to a subset of the workspace. sparse_slice_loaded: a sparse slice limited the loaded set. policy_limited_loaded: a policy projection hides one or more members. imported_root_only_loaded: the only loaded contributor is an imported root. warming_loaded / stale_loaded / evidence_missing_loaded: degraded readiness states the view discloses verbatim instead of pretending the full workspace is loaded.

workset_visibility_state.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#workset_scope_ref.visibility. Used on per-node / per-edge entries inside the view.

freshness_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#freshness. The freshness/provenance strip carries one freshness class per disclosed node entry.

stale_reason.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#stale_reason. Required when freshness_class != authoritative.

confidence_level.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#confidence_level. The view renders the rolled-up level verbatim and never silently upgrades.

anchor_drift_state.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#source_anchor_drift_state. Surfaces render the typed drift chip instead of a generic 'not available' message.

imported_root_status.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#imported_root_status. Imported entries carry the chip verbatim instead of folding into a local row.

extraction_or_inference_mode.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#extraction_or_inference_mode. The freshness/provenance strip discloses how the producer derived the value.

scope_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#workset_scope_ref.scope_class. The active scope is named once on the view header.

node_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#node_class. Node entries name the underlying node class so consumers can dispatch on it without re-deriving from the parent record.

ownership_card_section_class.description: Frozen ownership-card section vocabulary. Every ownership card lists which sections are present so a partial-evidence card can disclose which slots were unavailable instead of silently rendering blank tiles.

legend_section_class.description: Frozen legend-section vocabulary. The legend on a topology view names which honesty axes are disclosed; the surface never drops an axis silently.

filter_class.description: Frozen filter-class vocabulary. The view records which filters the user has applied so the export, review handoff, or list/table alternate render the same filtered set.

filter_engagement.description: Whether the filter is currently inactive, narrowing inclusively (only matching rows visible), or narrowing exclusively (matching rows hidden). The same filter never claims both inclusive and exclusive at once.

selected_values.description: Optional opaque token list naming which values the filter selects. Tokens are drawn from the matching closed vocabulary (node_class, edge_class, freshness, confidence_level, etc.).

filter_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note explaining the filter the surface renders verbatim.

filter_state_entry.description: One filter-state entry. The view records the full filter set so list/table alternates and exports apply the same narrowing.

tokens_displayed.description: Opaque token list (node-class names, edge-class names, freshness names, etc.) the legend displays. The surface never collapses two tokens into one swatch.

legend_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the legend renders verbatim.

hidden_by_workset_count.description: Count of nodes / edges excluded purely by the active workset's roots / patterns. Not collapsed into hidden_by_policy.

hidden_by_policy_count.description: Count of nodes / edges hidden by a policy projection in the active scope. The exact hidden ids never project.

not_loaded_count.description: Count of nodes / edges that exist by manifest but are not yet hydrated (warming index, deferred load).

evidence_missing_count.description: Count of nodes / edges whose target identity is known but whose evidence (lineage, anchor, citation) cannot be reconstructed from the current graph state.

imported_root_count.description: Count of disclosed entries whose imported_root_status is non-not_imported. The imported-root chip is rendered per entry; this aggregate count drives the freshness/provenance strip header.

hidden_count_summary.description: Hidden / partial / imported-root accounting for the view. Counts cross the boundary; the hidden member ids themselves never do (re-export of schemas/workspace/cross_repo_result_group.schema.json#hidden_count_summary plus an imported_root_count axis).

subject_kind.description: Which graph subject this strip entry summarises. Topology views render one entry per highlighted node / edge / card.

subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the node_id, edge_id, or card_id this strip entry summarises.

confidence_rollup_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the confidence_rollup_record (schemas/graph/confidence_rollup.schema.json) that produced rolled_up_confidence. Surfaces walk this ref to render the floor_reason / conflict_state chips.

anchor_drift_record_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to a source_anchor_drift_record (schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json) when the anchor_drift_state is not anchor_present_no_drift.

provenance_stamp_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provenance_stamp_record (schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json) the entry summarises.

strip_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the strip renders verbatim.

freshness_provenance_strip_entry.description: One freshness/provenance strip entry. The strip rides every topology view and ownership card so the same node renders the same freshness, confidence, anchor-drift, and imported-root chips on every surface — without inventing local-only certainty labels.

node_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the underlying graph_node_record.node_id. Topology views never mint a private node identifier.

display_label.description: Redaction-aware short label rendered alongside node_id_ref. Never substituted on links or citations.

policy_view_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to a policy_view_node_record when visibility_state == policy_hidden. The exact hidden node ids never project.

scope_ref.description: Optional opaque scope ref the node appears in. Re-exports the seed's workset_scope_ref.scope_id.

node_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the surface renders verbatim alongside the node entry.

topology_node_entry.description: One node entry inside the topology view. Reuses graph_node_record identity verbatim and pairs it with the per-node visibility / freshness / provenance / confidence / anchor-drift state every alternate (map / list / table / outline) renders identically.

edge_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the underlying graph_edge_record.edge_id.

topology_kind.description: Optional topology_kind from the seed's topology_edge_slot. Required when the edge is one of the seed's topology edges (depends_on, deployed_to, runs_in, hosted_by, produces_artifact, consumes_artifact, mirrors_upstream); null otherwise.

edge_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the surface renders verbatim alongside the edge entry.

topology_edge_entry.description: One edge entry inside the topology view. Reuses graph_edge_record identity verbatim. Topology surfaces MUST NOT mint a parallel topology edge record.

export_format_class.description: Frozen export-format vocabulary. Every export hook names exactly one format. Review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence packet exports preserve the full freshness/provenance strip and hidden-count summary; csv / dot / mermaid exports preserve at least the visible node and edge ids. Surfaces never claim 'the export carries the full state' if the chosen format drops imported-root chips or hidden counts.

preserves_hidden_counts.description: True when the export format preserves hidden_by_workset / hidden_by_policy / not_loaded / evidence_missing counts verbatim. Lossy formats (graphviz_dot, mermaid_flowchart, csv_*) MUST set this to false so the surface discloses the loss.

preserves_freshness_strip.description: True when the export carries the full freshness/provenance strip per entry. Lossy formats MUST set this to false.

preserves_imported_root_chip.description: True when the export preserves the per-entry imported_root_status chip.

manifest_ref.description: Optional opaque manifest ref the export will be filed under (review pack id, support bundle id, AI evidence packet id).

export_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the export hook renders verbatim. Surfaces preserve this on the export receipt.

handoff_class.description: Frozen review-handoff vocabulary. The view records which review-side handoffs are admissible so a topology surface and an impact explorer share the same handoff vocabulary instead of inventing per-surface synonyms.

ownership_node_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the underlying graph_node_record.node_id whose node_class == ownership_node. The card never mints a private ownership identifier; it reuses the seed's ownership identity.

display_label.description: Redaction-aware display label. Raw email addresses, handles, or real names never cross this boundary.

codeowners_rule_ref.description: Optional CODEOWNERS rule ref the ownership_node was resolved through.

active_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the active workset / scope artifact (schemas/workspace/workset_artifact.schema.json or schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json scope id). The ownership card never mints a parallel scope id.

sections_present.description: Closed list of sections the card actually rendered. A partial-evidence card discloses which sections were unavailable instead of silently rendering blank tiles.

freshness_provenance_strip.description: One or more strip entries summarising the freshness / provenance / confidence / anchor-drift state of the ownership identity itself plus the covered owned_by edges. The strip is required even on a partial-evidence card so the user sees why coverage is partial.

covered_node_count.description: Count of nodes the ownership_node currently covers in the active scope. Distinct from hidden_count_summary, which carries the excluded counts.

covered_node_id_refs.description: Optional list of node_id refs the ownership identity covers. The card MAY truncate this list for presentation; covered_node_count is the authoritative count.

missing_evidence_count.description: Count of covered nodes whose ownership evidence (CODEOWNERS rule, manual annotation, owned_by edge anchor) cannot be reconstructed in the current graph state. Drives the missing_evidence_summary section.

supporting_artifact_refs.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#supporting_artifact_ref. Support bundles and AI evidence packets walk this list to assemble a forensic packet.

card_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note the card renders verbatim.

ownership_card_record.description: One ownership card. The card reuses the underlying graph_node_record (ownership_node) and owned_by edges, projects a freshness/provenance strip from the same provenance / confidence / anchor-drift contract every other surface reads, and discloses partial-evidence cases through sections_present + missing_evidence_count instead of silently rendering blank tiles. A partial-evidence card sets loaded_scope_state to one of partial_workset_loaded / sparse_slice_loaded / policy_limited_loaded / imported_root_only_loaded / warming_loaded / stale_loaded / evidence_missing_loaded and discloses non-zero hidden_count_summary entries so the user can see why coverage is partial.

workspace_graph_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the workspace_graph_record this view was derived from.

active_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the active workset / scope artifact. The view never mints a parallel scope id.

legend_sections.description: Closed list of legend sections the view rendered. A view that disclosed an evidence-state chip on its edges MUST list edge_evidence_state_legend; a view with imported entries MUST list imported_root_legend; a view with policy-hidden members MUST list policy_overlay_legend.

filter_state.description: Per-view filter state. Empty array is admissible only when no filters were engaged; an empty filter_state on a partial render is non-conforming because the user cannot reproduce the narrowing.

node_entries.description: Ordered visible node entries. Empty array is admissible only when loaded_scope_state names why (partial_workset_loaded, policy_limited_loaded, imported_root_only_loaded, warming_loaded, stale_loaded, evidence_missing_loaded). An empty node_entries on fully_loaded is non-conforming.

edge_entries.description: Ordered visible edge entries. Empty array is admissible (a node-only view) only when the legend names node_class_legend without edge_class_legend.

freshness_provenance_strip.description: One or more strip entries the surface renders alongside the canvas / list / table. The strip is required so a topology surface cannot drop the freshness / confidence / anchor-drift / imported-root chips.

ownership_card_refs.description: Optional opaque refs to ownership_card_record card_ids the view links to. Topology family ownership_topology MUST list at least one ref so the card and the map share identity.

impact_explorer_view_refs.description: Optional opaque refs to impact_explorer_view_record view ids (schemas/graph/impact_reason.schema.json). The two surfaces share node and edge identity through these refs.

allOf.description: loaded_scope_state == fully_loaded MUST set every hidden_count_summary count field to 0 and every count_known field to true. The view never claims completeness while disclosing hidden rows.
loaded_scope_state == policy_limited_loaded MUST set hidden_by_policy_known true and policy_overlay_legend MUST appear on legend_sections.
loaded_scope_state == imported_root_only_loaded MUST set hidden_count_summary.imported_root_count > 0 and imported_root_legend MUST appear on legend_sections.
topology_family_class == ownership_topology MUST list at least one ownership_card_ref so the card and the map share ownership identity.

topology_map_view_record.description: One topology-map view record. Reuses graph_node_record / graph_edge_record identity verbatim; reuses the provenance / confidence / anchor-drift contract for the freshness/provenance strip; discloses loaded scope, hidden counts, imported-root counts, and legend / filter state so a partial or policy-limited workset is honest. Each view names exactly one topology_family_class so ownership / architecture-domain / runtime-domain / deployment-relation / dependency / generated-artifact / provider-resource / pack-mirror / imported-root / workset-scope maps stay distinguishable instead of flattening into one generic map. The presentation_mode axis covers map_canvas plus list_alternate / table_alternate / outline_tree_alternate so the same record drives every alternate without redefining identity.
